target.title: fix(ui): use catalog display names for agent models
target.body:
## Summary

- Prefer catalog display names for configured Agents/Control UI model options when the configured provider/model exists in the gateway model catalog.
- Keep intentional custom aliases visible by rendering them as `alias (Catalog Name)`.
- Avoid duplicating aliases that already match the full catalog display name.
- Use catalog names for raw catalog options too, instead of falling back to `id · provider`.
- Keep release-note context in the PR body only; this branch does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.

Closes #67988.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The Agents panel model selector should show catalog display names such as `Claude Opus 4.7` and `Claude Sonnet 4.6` instead of raw configured model ids like `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, while still preserving intentional custom aliases and avoiding duplicated labels such as `Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Claude Sonnet 4.6)`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, current PR head `42889570dad4dbb8c6968f5a8bafc8a6e4e4d2a2`, running the actual Agents model-option builder with gateway-model-catalog-shaped entries.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Rebased the PR branch onto current `upstream/main`, ran the focused UI regression suite, then ran a local `node --import tsx` probe against `buildModelOptions()` with configured Anthropic/OpenRouter catalog rows, default aliases, a custom alias, a full-label alias, and duplicate catalog display names.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the current-head local probe:

```text

  anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => Claude Sonnet 4.6 · anthropic

```

target.title: fix(gateway): silence mcp loopback conflict warn for typed unions
target.body:
## Summary

The MCP loopback flattens TypeBox union schemas into a single object so each tool publishes one parameter shape. When two variants declare the same field with different labels or descriptions, the flattener kept the first shape and emitted a warn. With many local tools using `Type.Union([Type.Object(...)])`, that warn fired on every tool listing and drowned real diagnostics.

The "first shape wins" fallback is the least surprising behavior here, so this PR promotes it to the documented contract instead of warning. Adds a regression test to lock the silent behavior in.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/gateway/mcp-http.schema.test.ts` — 1/1 passes; `logWarn` not called for typed-union conflicts
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck and lint green for the changed files
- [x] Manual: ran `openclaw gateway run` and listed tools; no spurious "conflicting schema definitions" lines in the gateway log

target.title: fix(memory-core): drop dreaming theme echoes from promotion candidates
target.body:
## Summary

Light Sleep / Deep Sleep phases write
\`\`\`
- Theme: \`X\` kept surfacing across N memories.
\`\`\`
into daily notes. The next dreaming cycle re-ingests the first line as a fresh candidate before the indented \`status: staged\` and \`recalls: 0\` rows land. The 5-field contamination check (\`hasNarrativeLead && hasConfidence && hasEvidence && hasStatus && hasRecalls\`) then misses the snippet, so the theme echo gets promoted into MEMORY.md and the cycle keeps recursing. Real workspaces accumulate dozens of these per agent (verified by inspection of multiple `~/.openclaw/workspace-*/memory/2026-04-*.md` files).

This PR adds a separate guard: when a snippet has a narrative lead AND matches the unique \`kept surfacing across N memories\` signature, mark it as contaminated even without status/recalls. The original 5-field check stays as additional safety.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts` — 45 tests pass, including the new "treats Light Sleep theme echoes as contaminated even without status/recalls" case
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck and tests green for extension lanes
- [x] Existing "does not treat ordinary candidate notes with daily-memory evidence as contaminated" still passes (regular candidates without the theme signature stay through)

target.title: Fix: Compact inbound metadata
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: Direct Telegram/chat messages can prepend repeated full Conversation info and Sender untrusted metadata JSON blocks before simple user text.
  - Why it matters: The repeated blocks add prompt noise and token overhead, and can make short direct-chat messages harder for the model to interpret.
  - What changed: Added optional messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct" to render direct-chat metadata as one compact untrusted line, while preserving full fenced metadata for groups and richer context.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Defaults remain unchanged; group chats, replies, forwards, locations, structured context, and history keep the existing full metadata format.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72704
- Related #62077
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: Inbound prompt assembly always used the full untrusted metadata block format for direct external-channel messages.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered safety and metadata presence, but not compact direct-chat prompt shape or stripping compact AI-facing metadata from user-visible history.
  - Contributing context (if known): Provider-level metadata fields are not portable across supported model providers, so the fix stays in prompt assembly/ config.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media- only.test.ts
  - Scenario the test should lock in: Compact direct metadata suppresses full sender/conversation JSON, group metadata stays full, config reaches prompt construction, and compact metadata is stripped from user-visible text.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the prompt builder, the call site, and the display/history sanitizer without requiring a live Telegram bot.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing inbound metadata tests covered full metadata behavior and safety escaping.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  New optional config: messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct".

  Default behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [direct chat message] -> [full conversation JSON + full sender JSON + body]

  After:
  [direct chat message + compact-direct] -> [one compact untrusted metadata line + body]
  [group/rich context] -> [existing full fenced metadata blocks]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
  - New/changed network calls? (No)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
  - Data access scope changed? (No)
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram/direct chat prompt assembly
  - Relevant config (redacted): messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct"

### Steps

  1. Configure messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct".
  2. Build inbound user context for a direct Telegram message.
  3. Compare prompt prefix against default/full mode and group-chat mode.

### Expected

  - Direct chats use one compact untrusted metadata line.
  - Sender name/username JSON block is not prepended in compact direct mode.
  - Groups and rich context retain full fenced metadata blocks.
  - Compact metadata does not appear in user-visible history.

### Actual

  - Matches expected after this PR.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

  What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - pnpm check:changed
      - pnpm build
      - pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts
        src/plugins/cli.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
      - pnpm config:schema:check
      - pnpm config:docs:check
      - codex review --base origin/main
  - Edge cases checked: direct vs group chats, compact metadata stripping, lookalike user text preservation, default config schema generation, wiki/memory CLI
    scope review finding.
  - What you did not verify: Live Telegram bot delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes)
  - Config/env changes? (Yes)
  - Migration needed? (No)
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Optional only. Set messages.inboundMetadataMode to "compact-direct" to enable the new direct-chat prompt format.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

  - Risk: Compact direct metadata could leak into user-visible history.
      - Mitigation: Added sanitizer support and tests for compact metadata stripping plus lookalike user text preservation.
  - Risk: Compacting metadata could remove context needed in groups or rich-message flows.
      - Mitigation: Compact mode applies only to direct chats; groups/replies/forwards/locations/history keep full metadata.

target.title: chore(ci): harden package-manager resolution
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: CI and repo scripts had several package-resolution escape hatches: `pnpm dlx`, docs publish `npm install --no-save --package-lock=false`, non-hash-pinned pre-commit hook refs, stale pnpm pins, and Python CI installs that only implicitly relied on repo-root uv config.
- Why it matters: These paths are package supply-chain risk multipliers. They let trusted CI jobs resolve code at runtime from mutable registries, tags, or one-off install commands, which widens the window where a compromised, newly published, or retagged package could execute before maintainers have time to review or react.
- What changed: This PR makes those fetch paths more boring and reviewable: repo scripts use lockfile-pinned dev dependencies instead of `pnpm dlx`; CI Python installs explicitly load repo uv policy; pre-commit hooks are pinned to immutable commits; stale pnpm pins are aligned; and pnpm/uv both enforce the same two-day minimum package age gate.
- Regression guardrail: Root `AGENTS.md` now records these CI/pre-commit and package-manager supply-chain rules so agentic coding does not reintroduce mutable refs or one-off installs.
- Lockfile note: The `pnpm-lock.yaml` diff is expected. Replacing `pnpm dlx` with `pnpm exec` means the tools previously fetched on demand now live as dev dependencies, so their transitive dependency graphs are reviewed and pinned in the lockfile. Most of the noise comes from `knip` pulling optional/native `oxc` packages for multiple platforms.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: These gaps came from convenience/bootstrap patterns that are common in CI: one-off `dlx` tools, ad-hoc docs installs, version-tag pre-commit hooks, and Python installs that happened to respect repo config only because they ran from the repo root.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no single guardrail preventing package-manager commands from resolving fresh packages outside the repo lockfile or configured age gate.
- Contributing context (if known): pnpm already had a minimum-release-age policy, but not every CI package fetch path was obviously tied to it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `Yes`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: This PR reduces package-fetch risk by making CI installs prefer lockfile/configured package-manager paths, immutable hook refs, and uv's repo-scoped `exclude-newer = "2 days"` policy. The lockfile changes are intentional: replacing `pnpm dlx` with `pnpm exec` requires adding those tools as dev dependencies so their transitive graphs are reviewed and pinned. Most of the lockfile noise comes from `knip` pulling optional/native `oxc` packages for multiple platforms.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm 10.33.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): CI/package-manager workflows
- Relevant config (redacted): pnpm `minimumReleaseAge: 2880`; uv `exclude-newer = "2 days"`

### Steps

1. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true`
2. `pnpm build`
3. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/ci.yml pnpm-workspace.yaml pyproject.toml`
4. `pnpm check:changed`

### Expected

- Install and build pass.
- Changed-file formatting passes.
- Package-manager hardening does not introduce build failures.

### Actual

- Install passed.

- Changed-file formatting passed.

target.title: fix(session): persist memory on auto daily/idle rollover
target.body:
## Summary

Auto-rollover (daily or idle stale-detection) takes a different code path than /reset and /new commands, so the bundled session-memory hook never sees a command:reset event and pre-reset transcripts go unwritten. Low-activity agents that get rolled over at 4am without ever compacting or typing a reset command silently lose their daily memory entry.

This PR fires the same internal command:reset hook on stale rollover with a commandSource of 'auto:daily' or 'auto:idle' so existing listeners (including the bundled session-memory handler) persist memory for automatic reset paths too. Guarded against firing on explicit /reset (handled by emitResetCommandHooks) and on fresh sessions.

target.title: gateway: register skills.uninstall RPC (mirror of skills.install)
target.body:
Fixes #69299.

Adds a `skills.uninstall` Gateway RPC method, mirroring `skills.install`, so operators can remove an installed ClawHub skill from the default agent workspace over the Gateway control plane.

## Changes

- Schema: `SkillsUninstallParamsSchema` in `src/gateway/protocol/schema/agents-models-skills.ts`, registered in `protocol-schemas.ts` and `types.ts`.
- Protocol: `SkillsUninstallParams` type + `validateSkillsUninstallParams` validator added to `src/gateway/protocol/index.ts` exports.
- Backend: `uninstallSkillFromClawHub()` in `src/agents/skills-clawhub.ts` removes `<workspaceDir>/skills/<slug>/` and drops the matching `.clawhub/lock.json` entry. Uses `normalizeTrackedSlug` (not the stricter `validateRequestedSlug`) so legacy non-ASCII tracked installs remain removable — symmetric with `updateSkillsFromClawHub`.
- Handler: `skills.uninstall` in `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.ts`, returning `INVALID_REQUEST` for schema/slug errors and `UNAVAILABLE` for backend errors.
- Registration: method name added to `src/gateway/server-methods-list.ts` and the `ADMIN_SCOPE` group in `src/gateway/method-scopes.ts`.
- Docs: new operator bullet in `docs/gateway/protocol.md`.

## Semantics

- Scope: `operator.admin` (same as `skills.install` / `skills.update`).
- Params: `{ slug: string; source?: "clawhub" }`.
- Idempotent: missing skill → `{ ok: true, removed: false, message: "Skill <slug> was not installed" }`.
- Shared bin dependencies are **not** removed — only the per-skill folder + lockfile entry.

## Tests

- 4 new Gateway handler tests in `src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts`: installed removal, idempotent not-installed (with `source: "clawhub"`), empty-slug rejection (`INVALID_REQUEST`), backend error surfaced as `UNAVAILABLE`.
- 6 new backend tests (real tmpdir fixtures) in `src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts`: removes skill dir + lockfile entry while leaving sibling installs intact, idempotent not-installed, removes lockfile entry when dir already gone, path traversal rejected, empty slug rejected, legacy non-ASCII tracked slug accepted.
- Gateway classification test `classifies every listed gateway method name` still passes — the new method is in `ADMIN_SCOPE`.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/skills-clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts` — 29/29 passed.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts` — 37/37 passed.
- `pnpm format`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`, `pnpm check:import-cycles`, `pnpm check:madge-import-cycles` all green (via `scripts/committer`).

target.title: fix(gateway): prefer current route delivery context
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes gateway agent delivery resolution so a concrete current request route (`channel`, `to`, `accountId`, `threadId`) wins over stale persisted session `deliveryContext` fields when the channels conflict.
- Clears stale route fields on channel switches when the new request does not provide replacement `to`/`accountId`/`threadId` values, avoiding mixed-channel routes.
- Preserves existing session-route behavior for normal follow-ups, `channel=last`, and same-channel existing-session routes.
- Compares same-channel targets through the channel parser so equivalent encodings do not clear stored threads.
- Populates `runContext.currentChannelId` from the resolved delivery target so downstream message-tool fallback inference sees the active route.
- Adds regression tests for the #68378 shape, channel-only stale-route clearing, same-channel partial updates, and canonical equivalent targets.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68378
- Related prior art: #22205 identified the missing `resolvedTo` -> `runContext.currentChannelId` propagation, but that PR is closed unmerged and does not address stale delivery-context precedence.
- Related but not duplicates: #45514, #65847, #33619, #39557, #51812, #52204 all describe adjacent multi-channel/stale-routing problems or other delivery paths; none is an open satisfactory PR for this gateway agent route-precedence bug.

## Root Cause / Diagnosis

`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` built `requestDeliveryHint` from the active gateway request, but then merged it with the stored session `deliveryContext` in a way that made persisted fields authoritative. That let stale persisted fields, such as Feishu `channel`/`to`, override a current Discord request before the agent command and session update were assembled. The same path also did not pass the resolved target into `runContext.currentChannelId`, so tool fallback routing could miss the current route even when `resolvedTo` was known.

A second edge case appears when the request changes only `channel`: route-field fallback must not pair a new channel with stale `lastTo`/`lastAccountId`/`lastThreadId` from the old channel.

A third edge case appears when the request and session refer to the same target with different channel-owned encodings, such as Slack `C1` and `channel:C1`: raw string comparison can falsely treat that as a target change and clear the stored thread.

## Fix

- Detect when a concrete request channel conflicts with the stored session delivery channel.
- In that cross-channel conflict case, let the current request delivery hint win and use persisted delivery metadata only as fallback for fields provided by the current route.
- Clear stale last-route fields when the current channel conflicts and no replacement field is available.
- For same-channel route conflicts, preserve unchanged target/account/thread fields unless the concrete target itself changes.
- Compare request/session targets using the channel parser before treating `to` as a target change, preserving stored threads for equivalent target spellings.
- For non-conflict cases, keep existing session delivery precedence so `channel=last`, normal follow-ups, and existing subagent routes behave as before.
- Pass `resolvedTo` into `runContext.currentChannelId` alongside the existing channel/account/thread context.
- Lock the behavior with regression tests that start from stale Feishu metadata, assert Discord wins or stale route fields are cleared, preserve partial same-channel updates, and keep Slack threads when `C1` and `channel:C1` are equivalent.

## Duplicate Sweep

Searched GitHub issues/PRs for combinations of `#68378`, Discord/Feishu wrong-channel delivery, stale `deliveryContext`, gateway agent delivery, `currentChannelId`, and multi-channel session routing. Closest results:

- #22205: overlapping `currentChannelId` propagation only; closed unmerged; no stale context precedence fix or regression for #68378.
- #35451: broader `chat.send`/cron canonical route derivation; closed unmerged; different call sites.
- #67508: target-agent bound account propagation for Matrix subagent spawns; adjacent account routing, different bug.
- #45514, #65847, #33619, #39557, #51812, #52204: related issue reports/bug class, not acceptable duplicate PRs for this exact gateway agent request-route overwrite.

Conclusion: no duplicate or already-satisfactory PR found for #68378.

## Attribution

- Thanks @Suidge for the #68378 report and follow-up logs that isolated Discord input being delivered through Feishu outbound.
- Thanks @Galygious for prior analysis in #22205 around forwarding `resolvedTo` into `runContext.currentChannelId`; this PR incorporates that same missing-context piece and adds the route-precedence fix needed for #68378.

## Testing

Fresh `origin/main` base: `64089fd15ed4ba8296612b1a622d11b5662c8b96`

- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "uses the current request route"`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "preserves the stored thread when the request target is canonically equivalent"`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-a.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts src/gateway/server.agent.subagent-delivery-context.test.ts`

The final command covers the CI shard failures observed after the first draft and now passes locally with 71 tests.

Scoped lint note: `pnpm lint -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` is currently blocked before linting these files by the existing `src/agents/skills/*` plugin SDK boundary type errors already present on `origin/main`.

## Review Follow-up

- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about clearing stale route fields on channel-switch merges in `5825aede6c7568f8cec2af2653d4851bf3296d78`.
- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about preserving unchanged same-channel route fields in `d8f4d09a343f30d82df2bdeea9541a707923a72d`.
- Addressed and resolved Codex review thread about canonical target comparison in `af7506771a0fd734024ecc2b51b23b7945f27b21`.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

The change narrows routing behavior to prefer the active request route only when it conflicts with stale persisted delivery metadata, and preserves stored thread context for equivalent same-channel target spellings.

target.title: fix(acp): treat missing cwd as stale bound session
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: a bound ACP session can stay attached after its persisted runtime working directory disappears, so retries keep failing against a dead session and the conversation stays wedged.
- Why it matters: affected conversations remain wedged until the stale binding state is manually cleared.
- What changed: during ACP dispatch, treat a generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` as stale when the bound session's persisted `runtimeOptions.cwd` can no longer be resolved on disk (`ENOENT`/`ENOTDIR`), then unbind the dead conversation. The signal is folded into the existing `isStaleSessionInitError` classifier and the existing `maybeUnbindStaleBoundConversations` dispatch helper — no new module, no parallel helper.
- Scope: dispatch-only. Two files: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` and its test.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- ACP stale-session classifier (`isStaleSessionInitError`) extended to accept the bound session's persisted cwd.
- Auto-reply ACP dispatch stale-binding cleanup (`maybeUnbindStaleBoundConversations`) passes the persisted cwd at the `runTurn` catch.
- Regression tests: generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` + missing persisted cwd unbinds; generic init failure + existing cwd does not.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #60720
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

The dispatch path already removes stale ACP bindings for selected ACP session init failures, but it classified staleness from the error message only. The live missing-cwd case surfaces as a generic spawn `ENOENT` wrapped as `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, whose message does not match the stale-message set. The reliable signal is the bound session's persisted `runtimeOptions.cwd`: when it no longer resolves to a directory, the binding is unrecoverable. Without checking it, the stale bound conversation survives the failed dispatch and keeps routing future turns into the dead ACP session.

## Implementation Notes

- Rebased onto current `main` after the `@openclaw/acp-core` runtime extraction. The fix now extends `main`'s existing in-file `maybeUnbindStaleBoundConversations`/`isStaleSessionInitError` instead of introducing a separate `src/acp/runtime/stale-session.ts` helper (which no longer fits the post-extraction layout). The earlier `extensions/memory-core` test shim is dropped — current `main` carries that mock contract natively.
- Staleness now requires **filesystem-confirmed** absence of the persisted cwd. The unbind cannot be triggered by an error-message string alone, which removes an over-removal vector (e.g. a transient tool error whose text mentions a missing working directory). `ENOENT` and `ENOTDIR` count as missing; a present-but-unreadable cwd (`EACCES`) is treated as present so a transient permission/mount issue never severs a live conversation.

## Maintainer Decision (session-state semantics)

This removes persisted bound-conversation state when a generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED` is paired with a filesystem-confirmed missing cwd — the recovery is correct for a permanently gone cwd, but it is session-state behavior worth explicit maintainer acceptance (per ClawSweeper's `merge-risk: session-state`). Two residual, deliberately out-of-scope behaviors to weigh:

- A transient filesystem outage (mount briefly returning `ENOENT`) on a still-valid cwd would unbind a live session. Mitigated by treating only `ENOENT`/`ENOTDIR` (not `EACCES`) as missing, but not eliminated.
- `unbind({ targetSessionKey })` removes all bindings for that session (pre-existing helper scope). For a shared-cwd session that is arguably correct, since the cwd is shared by all its bindings.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A bound ACP session whose persisted runtime `cwd` no longer exists should be treated as stale during dispatch cleanup, unbinding the affected conversation instead of keeping retries stuck on the same dead session.
- Real environment tested: Disposable local OpenClaw gateway from this PR checkout on NixOS Linux, Telegram DM via staging bot `@kesslerClawBot`, loopback gateway port `18794`, cron/heartbeats disabled. Gateway was stopped after the proof run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the disposable staging gateway from PR head `1ad568ba1e0db9051904eacee025ee36ec2f06ee`, sent a Telegram DM to `@kesslerClawBot` that routed through a bound ACP conversation whose persisted `runtimeOptions.cwd` pointed at `/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/missing-cwd-pr68843`, let ACP dispatch fail with generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, then inspected the post-run binding state.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted public proof artifact: https://gist.github.com/kesslerio/dbefdac0c5d5a5849997b6bf702047c6. Follow-up proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/68843#issuecomment-4558022819.

  ```txt
  2026-05-27T12:18:57.491-07:00 telegram: routed via bound conversation 8352721935 -> agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1
  2026-05-27T12:18:57.520-07:00 telegram inbound: chatId=8352721935 ... preview="[...] PR68843 patched missing cwd cleanup proof"
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.365-07:00 [plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend ready
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.400-07:00 dispatch-acp: removed 1 stale bound conversation(s) for agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1 after ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: Failed to spawn agent command: /run/current-system/sw/bin/codex-acp-openclaw
  2026-05-27T12:19:06.404-07:00 acp-dispatch: session=agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1 outcome=error code=ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED latencyMs=327 queueDepth=0 activeRuntimes=0
  ```

  Post-run binding state:

  ```json
  {
    "bindingCount": 0,
    "sessionKey": "agent:niemand-code:acp:binding:telegram:default:ecf1b7272077d0d1",
    "acpState": "error",
    "runtimeOptions": {
      "cwd": "/home/art/.local/state/openclaw-staging/disposable/missing-cwd-pr68843"
    }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the staging Telegram DM routed through the stale bound ACP conversation, ACPX reached the runtime backend, dispatch failed with generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`, and the stale bound conversation was removed because the persisted ACP cwd was missing. Post-run binding state showed `bindingCount: 0`.
- What was not tested: Production AlphaClaw was not used. This proof uses the disposable staging gateway and staging Telegram bot to avoid disrupting the live Telegram poller. A broader channel-neutral ACP proof was not run because the Telegram staging proof exercises the dispatch cleanup path directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the raw local `/home/art/...` gateway log is retained only as an operator audit pointer because it contains unnecessary routing identifiers and machine-specific details. Public proof is the redacted gist and copied transcript above.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current `main` only treated selected ACP init-failure messages as stale and did not inspect persisted ACP session `cwd` metadata for a generic `ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED`; ClawSweeper confirmed the source-level pre-fix path in its review comment.

## Tests and validation

Commands run on rebased head `79c9e2392d` (worktree off current `origin/main`):

```sh
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts -t "stale|cwd"
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

Results: formatting passed; focused stale/cwd dispatch suite passed (5 passed, 50 skipped — including the new missing-cwd-unbinds and existing-cwd-does-not-unbind cases); core test typecheck passed (exit 0).

## Security Impact

No new permissions, secrets handling, network calls, command execution surface, or data access scope. The behavioral expansion is limited to unbinding stale ACP conversations after an ACP init failure when the persisted ACP cwd is filesystem-confirmed missing.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config or migration required. The change affects only stale ACP init failure recovery.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted rebase onto current `main`, fold-in to the existing dispatch helper, structural/correctness/adversarial review hardening, validation, and PR-body update. The live staging proof above remains valid: it unbound via the generic `Failed to spawn agent command` failure plus the missing-cwd probe, which is exactly the path this rebased head preserves.

target.title: feat(anthropic): add advisor tool support [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for Anthropic's **advisor tool** (beta: `advisor-tool-2026-03-01`), a server-side tool that lets Claude consult a separate model instance during inference. Also adds generic handling for all server-side tool blocks (`server_tool_use`, `*_tool_result`) which were previously silently dropped by the transport stream.

Fixes several latent issues discovered along the way:
- `mapStopReason` no longer crashes on unknown stop reasons (now maps `compaction`/`model_context_window_exceeded` to `length`)
- All display text derived from API responses is now sanitized via `sanitizeTransportPayloadText`
- Advisor model config accepts provider-qualified refs (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`)

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #63930 (Feature request + design rationale + session log)
- [x] This PR adds a new feature

## Root Cause / Motivation

OpenClaw's Anthropic transport silently drops all server-side tool content blocks. This means:
- Users can't enable the new advisor beta tool
- `web_search` and `code_execution` tool invocations don't appear in transcripts
- `mapStopReason` throws on novel stop reasons, crashing the transport

This PR adds the missing handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### New: advisor tool support
Enable in `openclaw.json`:
```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "models": {
        "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6": {
          "params": { "advisor": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```
Or with a custom advisor model:
```json
{
  "params": {
    "advisor": { "enabled": true, "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6" }
  }
}
```

When the model consults the advisor, its output appears inline as `[Advisor] ...` text in the conversation.

### Changed: server-side tool block handling
- `server_tool_use` (advisor, web_search, code_execution) — silently dropped (these are invocation markers, not content)
- `*_tool_result` with readable text → text block with `serverToolDisplay: true` flag (visible in UI, skipped on API replay to keep transcript clean)
- `*_tool_result` without readable text → silently dropped
- `mapStopReason` now maps `model_context_window_exceeded` and `compaction` to `"length"` (previously would throw)

## Diagram

```text
Before:
  content_block_start { type: "server_tool_use" } → silently dropped
  content_block_start { type: "advisor_tool_result" } → silently dropped
  stop_reason: "compaction" → throws Error

After:
  server_tool_use → dropped (invocation marker)
  advisor_tool_result(advisor_result, text) → text block "[Advisor] ..." with serverToolDisplay:true
  advisor_tool_result(advisor_redacted_result) → text block "[Advisor output (encrypted)]"
  advisor_tool_result(advisor_tool_result_error) → text block "[Advisor error: <code>]"
  compaction → maps to "length"

convertAnthropicMessages:
  text blocks with serverToolDisplay: true → skipped on replay
  (keeps API transcript clean without polluting multi-turn context)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **Yes** — adds `advisor-tool-2026-03-01` beta header when advisor is enabled. Only applies to Anthropic API requests for Claude models.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** — advisor is server-side, runs inside Anthropic's infrastructure, no local execution
- Data access scope changed? **No**

### Security hardening included

- All display text derived from API responses is sanitized via `sanitizeTransportPayloadText` (prevents terminal escape sequence injection from compromised responses)
- Advisor model config uses strict regex validation (`/^claude-[a-z0-9.-]+$/`) — rejects non-Claude models with a warning instead of sending invalid requests
- `!Array.isArray()` guard on advisor config object
- Tool name collision detection — warns if a user-defined `advisor` tool exists instead of creating a duplicate
- `mapStopReason` hardening prevents throws on unknown API stop reasons

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: macOS (Darwin 24.6.0)
- Runtime: Node 24.8, pnpm 10.32
- Model/provider: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` via Anthropic API
- Config: `params.advisor: true` in `openclaw.json`

### Steps
1. Configure OpenClaw with Anthropic provider and advisor enabled
2. Run the agent pipeline: `openclaw agent --local --agent main -m "<complex question>"`
3. Observe `[Advisor] ...` text in the response for questions that trigger advisor consultation
4. Verify via debug logs: `advisor tool injected: model=claude-sonnet-4-6 totalTools=N`

### Expected
- Advisor tool is injected into every Anthropic request when `params.advisor: true`
- Advisor-tool-2026-03-01 beta header is present on requests
- When the model invokes the advisor, its output appears inline in the response
- Server-side tool block handling does not crash the transport
- `mapStopReason` handles all known stop reasons without throwing

### Actual
Matches expected. Live API validation scripts (`scripts/validate-*.ts`) confirm both `web_search` and `advisor` tool flows produce the expected SSE events.

## Evidence

- [x] **329 tests pass** — full `pnpm test` suite
- [x] **51/51 extension tests pass** — `pnpm test:extension anthropic`
- [x] **17 new transport tests** — advisor flow, mapStopReason resilience, server-side tool translation, redacted result handling
- [x] **6 new stream-wrapper tests** — config resolution, wrapper composition, name collision detection, non-Claude rejection
- [x] **1 new chat-content test** — `serverToolDisplay` extraction
- [x] **Live API validation** — two scripts confirm real Anthropic responses work correctly
- [x] **End-to-end agent pipeline verified** — `advisor tool injected: totalTools=27` confirmed via debug log

## Human Verification (required)

### Verified scenarios
- Live API call with `web_search` tool — observed `server_tool_use(name=web_search)` + `web_search_tool_result` + `code_execution_tool_result` with `encrypted_content` fields
- Live API call with `advisor` tool — observed `server_tool_use(name=advisor)` + `advisor_tool_result(advisor_result, text)` + text response
- Agent pipeline end-to-end — advisor tool visible in outgoing request payload (tool #27 of 27), model responds successfully
- Config validation — `advisor: { model: "gpt-4" }` correctly rejected with warning
- Config validation — `advisor: { model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6" }` accepted (provider prefix stripped)
- Duplicate tool detection — warns when a custom tool named `advisor` already exists

### Edge cases checked
- Unknown `stop_reason` values (e.g. `some_future_reason`) → maps to `"stop"` without throwing
- `compaction` and `model_context_window_exceeded` stop reasons → map to `"length"`
- `advisor_redacted_result` content → displays as `[Advisor output (encrypted)]`
- `advisor_tool_result_error` content → displays as `[Advisor error: <code>]`
- Untranslatable `web_search_tool_result` (array content) → silently dropped (no prompt pollution)
- Malformed advisor config (array, null, wrong type) → handled gracefully

### What I did not verify
- Multi-turn conversations where an encrypted advisor result must be replayed verbatim (documented as a known limitation — the API continues fine without it)
- Non-Anthropic provider fallback after an advisor turn (documented tradeoff — the `[Advisor]` text survives as normal conversation context, which preserves information for the fallback model)
- Advisor sub-inference cost/usage surfacing (deferred to follow-up)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — existing conversations without advisor config are unchanged
- Config/env changes? **No** — the `params` field is an open `z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())`, no schema change needed
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

### Known limitations (follow-up work)

1. **Encrypted content round-tripping**: `advisor_redacted_result.encrypted_content` and `web_search_tool_result` encrypted blobs are not sent back to the API on subsequent turns. The SDK says these "must be round-tripped verbatim," but live testing confirms the API continues fine without them — the model just loses encrypted context from prior server-side tool calls. Full round-tripping would require integration with the existing pruning/context-sizing pipeline (to avoid unbounded context growth from retained encrypted payloads). Deferred to a follow-up PR.

2. **Cost tracking**: Advisor sub-inference tokens (`usage.iterations` array) are not yet surfaced in `TransportUsage`. Adding them requires careful integration with the existing cost pipeline to avoid inflating `totalTokens` (which drives flush/compaction decisions). Deferred.

3. **Cross-provider fallback**: If a session switches from Anthropic to OpenAI/Google mid-conversation, `[Advisor] ...` text survives as normal assistant text in the replayed history. This is intentional — stripping it would lose conversation context for the fallback model. Documented in code comments.

### Risk: Anthropic changes advisor tool schema
- **Mitigation**: The beta header (`advisor-tool-2026-03-01`) is version-pinned. Future versions can be added alongside without breaking existing behavior.

### Risk: A user defines a custom tool named `advisor` that conflicts with our injection
- **Mitigation**: `createAnthropicAdvisorToolWrapper` checks for existing `advisor` tools before injection and warns on conflict instead of creating duplicates.

## AI-assisted

- [x] Mark as AI-assisted in the PR title or description — marked `[AI-assisted]` in title
- [x] Degree of testing: **fully tested** — `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, `pnpm test` (329 tests), `pnpm test:extension anthropic` (51 tests), live API validation scripts, end-to-end agent pipeline verification
- [x] Prompts/session logs — full session decision log posted to #63930
- [x] Confirm you understand what the code does — yes, design was iterated through 6 rounds of `codex review --base origin/main` with each finding addressed
- [x] `codex review --base origin/main` run locally — all findings resolved or documented as tradeoffs
- [x] Resolve or reply to bot review conversations — will do

Authored with Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code. All code reviewed and verified by a human.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

target.title: feat(gateway): wire coding tools into /tools/invoke HTTP surface
target.body:
> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `4fe6f873c9` (scope owner gate to built-in host-FS write tools; restacked on #85664 `d625c6de23`).** Mirrors #85664's read-side fix for `write`/`edit` and addresses the ClawSweeper plugin-fallback finding (`docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` write section). Restacked onto #85664's new base `d625c6de23` (which carries the read-side scoping); the write-side delta on top scopes the overstated "refused even when both config keys are set" wording to the **built-in** `write`/`edit` tools across the `hostFsWrite` config help (`src/config/schema.help.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` member comment (`src/config/types.gateway.ts`), the `hostFsWrite` bullet in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and the write section of `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` (prose, truth table, _Plugin name collision_ note). Per the resolver (`src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts`): for a non-owner caller the built-in is never materialized, but a same-named allowlisted plugin tool still resolves under the normal `gateway.tools.allow`/`deny` policy. Help strings / type comments / docs only — **no runtime code changed** (verified: the write-side commit `27deceb882..4fe6f873c9` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/types.gateway.ts`, and the two `docs/gateway/*.md` files — no `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, or `schema.labels.ts`), so the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `pnpm check:docs` (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links) and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `61e6f29bed` (config-help owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]/[P3]).** Restacked onto #85664's new base `7f9876c2b2`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `661d6838c0` is **config help / type docs** (verified: `git diff 661d6838c0 61e6f29bed` touches only `src/config/schema.help.ts` +4 and `src/config/types.gateway.ts` +25 — comment/string lines; every runtime file under `src/gateway/`, `src/security/`, `zod-schema.ts`, `schema.labels.ts` is byte-identical): the `hostFsWrite` `FIELD_HELP` string and the `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary + `hostFsWrite` member comment now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces, and the type summary is refreshed so it no longer calls `hostFsWrite` "future work". This directly resolves ClawSweeper **[P2]** (config help omits the owner gate) and **[P3]** (stale `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` summary). Because no runtime code changed, the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. Verified in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container: `schema.help.quality.test.ts` (23/23) and `pnpm check:docs` both green.

---

> **Update 2026-06-17 — head `661d6838c0` (docs owner-gate fix; resolves [P2]).** Restacked onto #85664's docs-updated read base `ca46eb9946`. The only delta vs the last-reviewed head `afe36ccf45` is **docs**: the `read` + `write`/`edit` truth tables in `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md` and the `hostFsRead`/`hostFsWrite` bullets in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` now document the **owner/admin sender** gate (`senderIsOwner === true`) the resolver enforces — directly resolving ClawSweeper's **[P2]** ("Document the owner gate for file-tool opt-ins"). No source files changed (verified: `git diff afe36ccf45 661d6838c0` touches only those two docs files), so `dist/` is byte-identical and the nine-case live proof below stands unchanged. `pnpm check:docs` passes in the `node:22-bookworm-slim` linux container (format clean, markdownlint 0 errors, MDX ok, 0 broken links).

---

Builds on #85664. Narrow extension of the broader direct-invoke umbrella tracked in #37131.

## Summary

Extends #85664's narrow read-only landing to also expose `write` and `edit` over the gateway direct-invoke surfaces (HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` AND SDK RPC `tools.invoke`, which share the `resolveGatewayScopedTools` resolver). Same **triple-key owner-gated** shape as #85664's `read`: a surface-specific class opt-in **plus** the per-tool name in `gateway.tools.allow` **plus** `senderIsOwner === true`, all on the `http` surface.

## Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base (head `afe36ccf456f`, base upstream `023ce6e96c38`)

This branch was rebuilt on top of #85664's current head `5ca72af1ba` (itself rebased onto current upstream `023ce6e96c38`) and the write/edit extension re-applied with the **same owner-gate**. This directly resolves the prior ClawSweeper [P1].

**Bot [P1] (resolved):** upstream commit `02182d5a3031` ("refactor: remove sender owner tool gating") deleted the `ownerOnly` enforcement helper, so the earlier `ownerOnly: true` metadata tag this PR added became dead code — a trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller could have reached an allowlisted mutating tool with no owner check. **Fix:** this PR no longer relies on that tag. `write`/`edit` are materialized into the direct-invoke candidate set **only when the resolved sender is owner** (`senderIsOwner === true`, evaluated at resolve time in `tool-resolution.ts`), exactly mirroring #85664's read gate. A trusted-proxy `operator.write` caller is now refused even with both config keys set (proven below, cases 2 & 4).

## Triple-key gating

To reach a write-class tool on the direct-invoke surface, ALL must hold:

| Key | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `gateway.tools.directInvoke.hostFsWrite: true` | Class-level opt-in for the whole write family. | `false` |
| `gateway.tools.allow: [...]` includes the tool name | Per-tool enablement. | not present |
| `senderIsOwner === true` | Caller resolves to owner/admin (trusted-proxy `operator.admin`, or shared-secret bearer). | — |

Truth table (tool name `T` ∈ `{write, edit}`, surface = `http`):

| `allow` ∋ `T` | `hostFsWrite` | sender owner | `T` reachable |
|---|---|---|---|
| no  | no  | —   | ❌ |
| yes | no  | yes | ❌ (class opt-in off; built-in not materialized; `dangerous_allow` audit fires) |
| no  | yes | yes | ❌ (not in allow) |
| yes | yes | **no**  | ❌ (**owner gate** — refused) |
| yes | yes | yes | ✅ |

## Scope

- **Adds:** `write`, `edit` to direct-invoke (behind `hostFsWrite: true` + allow + owner).
- **Inherits from #85664:** `read` via `hostFsRead: true` + allow + owner.
- **`apply_patch`:** present in `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY` for defense-in-depth, but the coding-tools factory produces no `apply_patch` entry, so it is never materialized (proven below, case 7).
- **NOT exposed:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` — RCE-class; out of scope for this PR.

## Audit

- `gateway.tools_invoke_http.host_write_allow` fires only when BOTH `hostFsWrite: true` AND a write-class name is in `allow` (mirrors `host_read_allow`); escalates to `critical` on non-loopback bind.
- `dangerous_allow` suppression for `write`/`edit` is **source-aware** — suppressed only when the `hostFsWrite` class opt-in is active; `apply_patch` is never suppressed.

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — adds `hostFsWrite?: boolean` to `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — `hostFsWrite` schema entry
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`, `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — field help + label
- `src/security/dangerous-tools.ts` — adds `"write"`, `"edit"` to `DEFAULT_GATEWAY_HTTP_TOOL_DENY`
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.ts` — owner-gated write/edit materialization; coding built-ins win plugin name collision when class opt-in active
- `src/security/audit-gateway-config.ts` — `host_write_allow` finding; source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression
- `src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` — write coding-tool collision precedence (owner→built-in, non-owner→plugin, opt-in-off→plugin)
- `src/security/audit-gateway-tools-http.test.ts` — `host_write_allow` dual-key + source-aware `dangerous_allow` suppression cases (incl. `apply_patch` never suppressed)
- `src/agents/agent-tools.ts` — coding-tool factory plumbing
- `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, `docs/gateway/tools-invoke-http-api.md`, `docs/gateway/security/audit-checks.md` — `hostFsWrite` opt-in, triple-key truth tables, `host_write_allow` catalog row

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Extend #85664's owner-gated direct-invoke `read` to also expose `write` and `edit` over the Gateway HTTP `POST /tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke` surfaces, behind the same triple key (class opt-in `directInvoke.hostFsWrite` + per-tool `gateway.tools.allow` + `senderIsOwner`). Re-stacked on #85664's owner-gated read base so write/edit materialize only for an owner sender — resolving the prior [P1] where upstream `02182d5a3031` made the old `ownerOnly` tag dead code.

**Real environment tested:** Built fresh from head `afe36ccf456f` inside a `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64-native container (full runtime build → `dist/index.js`; SDK → `packages/sdk/dist/index.mjs`). Each case boots a live `openclaw gateway run` on a real loopback socket; owner = trusted-proxy `operator.admin` (and a shared-secret bearer token for the SDK case), non-owner = trusted-proxy `operator.write`. Target file states are re-read from disk after each call to prove real host mutation or its absence.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** `PROOF_HEAD=afe36ccf456f bash .proof-63919.sh` inside the container — boots nine gateway configs and dispatches `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` over HTTP `/tools/invoke` and SDK `tools.invoke`, asserting the HTTP status and re-reading each target file from disk.

**Evidence after fix:** live terminal output captured from the container at head `afe36ccf456f`:

```
=== HEAD: afe36ccf456f ===

### LIVE PROOF (HTTP trusted-proxy surface) ###

--- 1 W-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 24 bytes to /work/.proof63/w1.txt"}]}}
  file=OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f
--- 2 W-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=PRESEED
--- 3 E-OWNER-ENABLED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /work/.proof63/e3.txt."}],"details":{"diff":" 1 ALPHA\\n-2 BETA\\n+2 GAMMA-afe36ccf456f", ...}}}
  file=ALPHA / GAMMA-afe36ccf456f
--- 4 E-NONOWNER-DENIED (allow=[edit] hostFsWrite=true, operator.write, tool=edit) ---
  [HTTP operator.write] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: edit"}}
  file=ALPHA / BETA  (unchanged)
--- 5 W-DENIED-no-opt-in (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=false, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 6 W-DENIED-no-allow (allow=[] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=write) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: write"}}
  file=<absent>
--- 7 APPLYPATCH-denied (allow=[apply_patch] hostFsWrite=true, operator.admin, tool=apply_patch) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 404 {"ok":false,"error":{"type":"not_found","message":"Tool not available: apply_patch"}}
--- 8 READ-REGRESSION (allow=[read] hostFsRead=true, operator.admin, tool=read) ---
  [HTTP operator.admin] 200 {"ok":true,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"READ-BASE-OK-afe36ccf456f\\n"}]}}

### LIVE PROOF (SDK token surface — shared-secret bearer = owner) ###

--- 9 SDK-W-OWNER (allow=[write] hostFsWrite=true, token bearer, tool=write) ---
  [SDK token] {"ok":true,"toolName":"write","output":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"Successfully wrote 28 bytes to /work/.proof63/w9.txt"}]},"source":"core"}
  file=SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f

=== PROOF COMPLETE: HEAD=afe36ccf456f ===
```

Independent re-read of persisted file states after the run: `w1.txt`=`OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`, `w2.txt`=`PRESEED` (non-owner write left it untouched), `e3.txt`=`ALPHA/GAMMA-afe36ccf456f`, `e4.txt`=`ALPHA/BETA` (non-owner edit left it untouched), `w5.txt` and `w6.txt` absent (never created), `w9.txt`=`SDK-OWNER-WROTE-afe36ccf456f`.

**Observed result after fix:** All nine cases behaved as specified — owner writes/edits land and mutate the host file; a non-owner `operator.write` caller is refused `write`/`edit` (404) with the target file left untouched; missing class opt-in or missing allow entry both deny; `apply_patch` is never materialized; and the inherited `read` path still works:

| # | Case | Config | Caller | HTTP/SDK | File state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | write owner | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 wrote 24 bytes | written |
| 2 | write **non-owner** | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 3 | edit owner | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 200 replaced 1 block | edited |
| 4 | edit **non-owner** | allow=[edit], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.write` | 404 not_found | **unchanged** (owner gate) |
| 5 | write no opt-in | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=false | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 6 | write no allow | allow=[], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | never created |
| 7 | apply_patch | allow=[apply_patch], hostFsWrite=true | `operator.admin` | 404 not_found | factory never materializes it |
| 8 | read regression | allow=[read], hostFsRead=true | `operator.admin` | 200 + contents | #85664 base intact |
| 9 | write owner (SDK) | allow=[write], hostFsWrite=true | token bearer | ok=true wrote 28 bytes | written |

Supplemental (not a substitute for the live proof above), all in the same `node:22-bookworm-slim` arm64 container at head `afe36ccf456f`: unit tests — **160 passed** across the gateway test projects (`tools-invoke-http` 42 + `tool-resolution` 5 + `audit-gateway-tools-http` 19, ×3 projects = 7 file-projects); `lint` (oxlint, 0/0), `tsgo:core` (0), `check:test-types` (0), and `plugin-sdk-surface-report` vitest (4/4). **`checks-node-core-fast` / plugin-SDK surface budget:** this PR adds `hostFsWrite?` as a *field* on the existing `GatewayDirectInvokeOptIns` type — **zero new top-level exports** — so `plugin-sdk-surface-report --check` passes on the `+1` budget already carried by #85664's base commit (`config-types` 416 / public 10285 / public-fn 5164 / deprecated 3238). No further budget change in this PR.

**Not tested:** `exec`, `process`, `spawn`, `shell` (RCE-class; out of scope for this PR and never exposed by the direct-invoke factory).

target.title: feat(google): add Google Vertex AI provider with ADC auth and global endpoint routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Registers a new `google-vertex` provider in the Google plugin that routes to `aiplatform.googleapis.com` using Application Default Credentials (ADC), separate from the existing `google-gemini-cli` OAuth path
- Adds `vertex-region.ts` for region/project/baseUrl resolution (env vars + ADC file fallback), with `global` location producing the unprefixed `aiplatform.googleapis.com` endpoint
- Adds `vertex-provider-catalog.ts` with the Gemini 3.x model catalog for Vertex AI
- Updates the Google transport stream to detect Vertex endpoints and construct the correct project/location URL path (`/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/publishers/google/models/{model}:streamGenerateContent`)
- Exchanges ADC refresh token for a Bearer token at request time, handling the pi-ai `"<authenticated>"` sentinel that was previously sent as a literal API key causing 401s
- Captures `thought_signature` from Gemini 3 thinking-mode tool call responses so multi-turn conversations replay correctly (fixes 400 `INVALID_ARGUMENT` on second turn)
- Adds `GCP_VERTEX_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_MARKER` sentinel and registers `google-vertex` in `openclaw.plugin.json`

Closes #49039
Closes #56253
Closes #58775
Closes #60736

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-region.test.ts` — 20 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/google/vertex-provider-catalog.test.ts` — 7 cases, all pass
- [x] `pnpm test src/infra/gemini-auth.test.ts` — 5 cases, all pass
- [x] Live: `google-vertex/gemini-3-flash-preview` with `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=global` and `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT` set — correct Vertex URL, Bearer auth, successful response

target.title: feat(openresponses): return reasoning/thinking content in /v1/responses output
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The `/v1/responses` endpoint does not return model reasoning/thinking content. When models produce chain-of-thought reasoning (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI o-series, Gemini thinking, Ollama thinking models), the reasoning output is silently discarded by the HTTP API. Callers have no way to observe model reasoning through the OpenResponses interface.
- **Why it matters:** The [Open Responses spec](https://www.openresponses.org/reference) defines `type: "reasoning"` as a first-class output item (`ReasoningBody`) and `response.reasoning.delta`/`response.reasoning.done` as streaming events. Not implementing these means OpenClaw's OpenResponses endpoint is incomplete relative to the spec, and API callers (dashboards, orchestrators, observability tools) cannot access reasoning data.
- **What changed:** The endpoint now captures reasoning content from agent thinking events and returns it as `type: "reasoning"` output items (non-stream) or `response.reasoning.delta`/`response.reasoning.done` SSE events (stream). When no assistant text is produced but reasoning exists, the reasoning text is used as a fallback response to avoid empty replies from thinking-only models.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No config changes required. No new dependencies. Existing behavior for non-reasoning models is unchanged. The `onReasoningStream` callback path for channel-specific reasoning delivery (Discord, Telegram, etc.) is unmodified. This change only adds reasoning to the HTTP API surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #54496
- Related #44513
- Related #8364
- Partially addresses #31449 (OpenResponses reasoning output/events only; full tool I/O/context parity still pending)
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A — this is a new feature implementing an existing spec item.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Non-streaming responses

When reasoning is produced, the response `output` array now includes a `type: "reasoning"` item before the assistant message:

```json
{
  "output": [
    {
      "type": "reasoning",
      "id": "reasoning_abc123",
      "content": "Let me think about this step by step..."
    },
    {
      "type": "message",
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": [{ "type": "output_text", "text": "The answer is 42." }]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Streaming responses

Two new SSE event types are emitted during streaming:

- `response.reasoning.delta` — cumulative reasoning text as it streams
- `response.reasoning.done` — final reasoning text when complete

### Fallback behavior

When a model produces reasoning but no assistant text (common with thinking-only model configurations), the reasoning content is used as the response text instead of returning "No response from OpenClaw."

## Diagram (if applicable)

```
Model produces reasoning + answer:

  [thinking events] ──→ collect reasoning ──→ output[0]: reasoning item
  [assistant events] ──→ collect text     ──→ output[1]: assistant message

Model produces reasoning only (no assistant text):

  [thinking events] ──→ collect reasoning ──→ output[0]: reasoning item
  (no assistant text)                     ──→ output[1]: assistant = reasoning fallback
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No — reasoning content was already produced by the model, it was just being discarded

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.4 (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime: Node 22, Bun 1.2
- Model: any reasoning-capable model (Claude, o-series, Gemini thinking)

### Steps

1. Send a POST to `/v1/responses` with a reasoning-capable model
2. Observe the response `output` array

### Expected

Reasoning content appears as `type: "reasoning"` output item.

### Actual

Same as expected.

## Evidence

- [x] `pnpm build` green
- [x] `pnpm check` green (lint, format, types)
- [x] `src/gateway/openresponses-http.test.ts` — 12/12 tests pass
- [x] `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.*` — 21/30 pass (9 pre-existing upstream failures, verified same count on clean `upstream/main`)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: non-stream reasoning collection, stream reasoning events, fallback from thinking to text, cleanup on error
- Edge cases: no reasoning produced (no-op), reasoning-only response (fallback), tool-call + reasoning combined output
- Not verified: production load, all provider-specific thinking formats

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — adds new output items, does not remove or change existing ones
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: reasoning content could be large (10k+ chars) for complex prompts, increasing response payload size
  - Mitigation: This mirrors the model's actual output; callers can ignore the reasoning item if not needed
- Risk: double `emitAgentEvent` calls (raw + formatted) for channel-streaming reasoning mode
  - Mitigation: The raw-only event fires first for HTTP capture; the formatted event fires only when `streamReasoning` is active. HTTP consumers filter by `rawText` presence.


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/AssistantTextParserTests.swift`
- Scenario the test should lock in: session-scoped streaming updates must survive bootstrap/history churn and preserve stable parsed output/visibility while tokens stream.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug lives in shared chat state/parsing logic, so package-level tests cover the core behavior without requiring a full end-to-end harness.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): targeted parser/view-model tests were added/expanded for this branch.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- iOS chat streaming stays attached to the active session instead of a synthetic placeholder lane.
- Streaming text/layout behaves more stably during initial load/history refresh.
- Visible flicker/churn from repeated parse/visibility mismatches is reduced.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local dev environment
- Runtime/container: shared OpenClawKit package + iOS app build
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): iOS chat UI / streaming flow
- Relevant config (redacted): local iOS simulator build

### Steps

1. Build the shared package and iOS app from this branch.
2. Open a chat session and trigger a streaming reply.
3. Observe initial history/bootstrap plus active token streaming.

### Expected

- Streaming binds to the active session.
- Bootstrap/history churn does not destabilize the active stream.
- Layout remains visually stable while tokens arrive.

### Actual

- Matched expected in targeted tests and local build validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests`
  - `swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests`

- Edge cases checked:
  - stream acceptance during bootstrap/history load
  - parser stability under repeated streaming updates
- What you did **not** verify:

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
  - All 19 review threads (P1/P2 from `chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]` + `copilot-pull-request-reviewer`) resolved in this update.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: chat-state timing bugs can reappear under bootstrapping or external-final sync edge cases.
  - Mitigation: this patch scopes state to the active session and adds targeted parser/view-model coverage around the churn paths.



## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: iOS/shared OpenClawChatUI streaming state remains scoped to the active session, load recovery remains idempotent after a failed initial bootstrap plus successful manual refresh, and streaming text/tool state does not get cleared by a later view `load()` call.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS local dev environment, Xcode 26.5 SDKs, Swift Testing 1902, OpenClawKit shared package and generated iOS Xcode project from this PR head `591a724df2531d373fc8603f2faa323fc93bc053`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter ChatViewModelTests
swift test --package-path apps/shared/OpenClawKit --filter AssistantTextParserTests
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml
xcodebuild -project apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj -scheme OpenClawChatUI -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO build
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
ChatViewModelTests: Test run with 83 tests in 1 suite passed after 5.550 seconds.
AssistantTextParserTests: Test run with 7 tests in 1 suite passed after 0.001 seconds.
xcodegen generate --spec apps/ios/project.yml: Created project at /Users/eulices/projects/openclaw-pr50483/apps/ios/OpenClaw.xcodeproj
xcodebuild OpenClawChatUI generic iOS Simulator: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The recovered-load regression is covered by `successful refresh after failed load makes later load idempotent`, which passed after confirming a failed first bootstrap can recover through manual refresh and a later `load()` does not issue another history request or clear the active stream. The shared `OpenClawChatUI` target also built successfully for iOS Simulator from the generated project.
- **What was not tested**: Full `OpenClaw` app scheme build was attempted but blocked by local Xcode environment missing watchOS 26.5 for the embedded Watch app; the directly changed shared chat UI target was built instead. Full repository CI matrix was left to GitHub.

target.title: fix(cron): prevent scheduler death when startup catch-up fails
target.body:
## Summary

When runMissedJobs() throws during start(), the timer was never armed, silently killing the cron scheduler until the next gateway restart.

## Root Cause

The start() function in src/cron/service/ops.ts calls runMissedJobs() outside of any try/catch, followed by the locked() block that calls armTimer(state). If runMissedJobs() throws (e.g. an isolated agent turn fails during startup catch-up), execution never reaches armTimer(), and the scheduler is permanently dead.

## Changes

1. **Guard armTimer in start()** - Wrap runMissedJobs() in try/catch so the timer is always armed
2. **Zombie detection in onTimer()** - Clear stale runningAtMs markers at runtime (threshold: 2x job timeout or 2 min) so jobs recover without restart
3. **Tests** - Verify timer stays armed on catch-up failure and stale markers are cleared

Fixes #67854
Related: #59056, #60799
